audienceSections.normal: In Aki and Yuki meeting for the first time, you will use [[grammar:N + です]]. Put [[grammar:です]] after a name or role for a polite identity statement. ゆきです。教師です。 means ‘I’m Yuki. I’m a teacher.’ Japanese can leave out ‘I’ when it is clear, so do not add 私 to every line.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter presents [[grammar:N + です]] in Aki and Yuki meeting for the first time. The polite copular predicate consists of a nominal plus です, with the discourse subject omitted. 教師 is appropriate self-occupation vocabulary; 先生 remains appropriate for address or reference to another teacher. よろしくお願いします carries a relationship-opening pragmatic meaning rather than a literal request alone.

audienceSections.normal: Put [[grammar:です]] after a name or role for a polite identity statement. ゆきです。教師です。 means ‘I’m Yuki. I’m a teacher.’ Japanese can leave out ‘I’ when it is clear, so do not add 私 to every line.
audienceSections.expert: The polite copular predicate consists of a nominal plus です, with the discourse subject omitted. 教師 is appropriate self-occupation vocabulary; 先生 remains appropriate for address or reference to another teacher. よろしくお願いします carries a relationship-opening pragmatic meaning rather than a literal request alone.

audienceSections.normal: Use the readings in the line-by-line help for unfamiliar kanji. Read the Japanese sentence first, then check the natural English meaning.
audienceSections.expert: The reading uses standard polite です／ます style with discourse-supported argument omission. Kana readings identify the attested lexical forms without replacing the Japanese script.

breakdown.normal:
Read each exact japanese line, then check its natural English meaning. Watch for [[grammar:N + です]] where it appears.

- はじめまして。 — Nice to meet you.
- こんにちは。 — Hello.
- あきです。学生です。 — I am Aki. I am a student.
- ゆきです。教師です。 — I am Yuki. I am a teacher.
- よろしくお願いします。 — Nice to meet you; I look forward to getting along with you.
- こちらこそ、よろしくお願いします。 — Likewise; I look forward to getting along with you.
breakdown.expert:
These exact reading lines provide the distributional evidence for [[grammar:N + です]]. The grammar section above analyzes the relevant morphology and syntax.

- はじめまして。 — Nice to meet you.
- こんにちは。 — Hello.
- あきです。学生です。 — I am Aki. I am a student.
- ゆきです。教師です。 — I am Yuki. I am a teacher.
- よろしくお願いします。 — Nice to meet you; I look forward to getting along with you.
- こちらこそ、よろしくお願いします。 — Likewise; I look forward to getting along with you.

readingItems.surface: よろしくお願いします
readingItems.reading: よろしくおねがいします
readingItems.evidence: よろしくお願いします。
こちらこそ、よろしくお願いします。
